params.description: Image A: Stacks of 10 dollar bills, labeled A.
Image B: Ruler marked in 10s of centimeters, labeled B.
Image C: Counting items one by one, labeled C.

beats.text: Scale of 10 is useful when numbers are bigger. Where might you see counting by 10s outside of math class?
Which situations would work well with a scale of 10? Select all that apply.

validator.description: Student selected A and B, both involve counting by 10s

beats.text: Money and measuring often use 10s. That's why scale of 10 feels natural for bigger amounts.

elements.description: Image A: Stacks of 10 dollar bills.
Image B: Ruler marked in 10s of centimeters.
Image C: Counting items one by one.

beats.text: You already know how to count by 10s. Bar graphs use the same skill you use with money and measuring.
